title: Acetazolamide
buzzwords: Weak diuretic with systemic acid-base effects
Oral or IV sulfonamide-type agent
Prophylaxis before rapid high-altitude ascent
Induces compensatory metabolic acidosis to drive hyperventilation
Lowers CSF production in pseudotumor cerebri
Adjunct for refractory seizures
Systemic carbonic anhydrase inhibitor of choice for acute glaucoma
Prototype -zolamide given PO/IV

title: Methazolamide
buzzwords: Oral carbonic anhydrase inhibitor
Reduces aqueous humor production from ciliary body
Used when systemic therapy is preferred over eye drops
Lowers intraocular pressure in open-angle glaucoma
Sulfonamide-derived -zolamide drug
Alternative oral agent when acetazolamide poorly tolerated
PO-only CAI indicated solely for glaucoma
Oral methazolamide for chronic glaucoma

title: Dorzolamide
buzzwords: Eye-drop carbonic anhydrase inhibitor
Minimizes systemic adverse effects via local delivery
Preferred for chronic open-angle glaucoma
Suppresses ciliary body HCO3- and aqueous humor
Often combined with timolol in fixed eye drop
Sulfonamide-based -zolamide for ocular use
First topical CAI approved for glaucoma
Topical dorzolamide ophthalmic solution

title: Brinzolamide
buzzwords: Topical ophthalmic suspension for elevated IOP
Local CAI sparing systemic acidosis
Long-term outpatient glaucoma therapy
Decreases aqueous humor by blocking ciliary CA
Milky white eye-drop formulation
Sulfonamide-derived -zolamide for the eye
Often paired with brimonidine in combination drop
Topical brinzolamide ophthalmic suspension
